raw_text: ABBOTT, A. W., C.B.E. (1949).—b. 1893 ; ed. Blundell’s ; entd. C.A. off. after compet. exam., 1912 ; mil. serv., 1917-19; lieut.; asst. hd., fin. dept., 1929 ; dep. hd., 1933 ; hd., 1938 ; sec., E.A. currency bd., 1930-38 ; dep. ch. clk., 1945 ; ch. clk. (estab. offr.), 1948.

ABBOTT, C. T.—b. 1897 ; ed. Portora Royal Sch., Enniskillen ; barrister-at-law, Gray’s Inn ; mil. serv., 1914-19 ; res. mag., crown coun., Ken., 1930 ; pres. dist. ct., Cyp., 1932 ; solr.-gen., S.L., 1938 ; puisne judge, Nig., 1944 ; Mal., 1950.

ABBOTT, M. J.—b. 1906 ; ed. King’s Sch., Canterbury ; barrister, Gray’s Inn ; mil. serv., 1941-45, lieut.; asst. crown solr., H.K., 1936 ; secon. to Ethiopian Govt. as pres., high ct., 1946 ; puisne judge, Nig., 1950.

ABBOTT, T. K.—b. 1912 ; ed. King’s Sch., Parramatta, Univ. Sydney and Sydney Hosp.; mil. serv., 1941-45, maj.; M.O.H., H.K., 1940 ; resig. and re-apptd. Borneo, 1945 ; ag. D.M. and H.S., Sarawak, 1945 ; M.O.H., Tang., 1947 ; S.M.O., Sey., 1948 ; D.D.M.S., N. Borneo, 1951.
ABELL, Sir Anthony, K.C.M.G. (1952), C.M.G.—b. 1906 ; ed. Repton and Magdalen Coll., Oxford ; cadet, Nig., 1929 ; admin. offr. cl. II, 1946 ; cl. I, 1948 ; gov. and c.in-c., Sarawak, 1950.

ABERNETHY, J. S.—b. 1907 ; ed. Sedbergh Sch., Aberdeen Gram. Sch., Aberdeen and Edin. Univs.; chartered co., N. Borneo ; legal advr., comsnr. of lands, prot. of labr. (incl. inspr., schs.), 1936 ; food contri. (in addn.), 1941 ; sessions judge, B.M.A. (B. Borneo), 1946 ; comsnr., lands, 1947 ; sessions judge, 1948 ; res. mag., Tang., 1949 ; puisne judge, 1951.
ABRAHAM, A. L., C.P.M.—b. 1908 ; ed. Southend High Sch.; police, Pal., 1930 ; supt., Barb., 1938 ; Fiji, 1944 ; B. Hond., 1950 ; comsnr., Gib., 1953.
ABRAHAMS, A. R.—b. 1908 ; ed. J’ca Coll.; apptd. J’ca, 1927 ; pensions offr., 1944 ; asst. sec., 1950 ; prin., 1952 ; sec. to various comtees.

ABRAHAMS, Rt. Hon. Sir Sidney, P.C. (1941), Kt. Bach. (1936), Q.C.—b. 1885 ; ed. Bedford Modern Sch. and Emmanuel Coll., Camb.; called to Bar, Middle Temp., June, 1909 ; hon. Bencher, 1948 ; Midland circuit ; town mag., Zanz., 1915 ; cr. coun., Ken., 1915 (did not proceed) ; secon. Mespot. civ. admin., 1920 ; advoc-gen. and registr. of companies, Baghdad, 1920 ; pres., ct. of first instance, ct. of sessions and ct. of criminal appl., Basrah, 1921 ; atty-gen., Zanz., 1922 ; Uga., 1925 ; G.C., 1928 ; ch. just., Uga., 1933 ; Tang., 1934 ; Ceylon, 1936-39 ; member of jud. comtee. of privy cl., 1941 ; jt. edr., Zanz. law rept., 1868-1918 ; sole edr., 1918-22 ; 2nd cls. Order of Brilliant Star of Zanz., 1924 ; addl. asst. leg. advr., C.O., 1939 ; sole comsnr. to enquire into Nyasaland land problems, 1946 ; edited (with A. Abrahams) Dagens Nyheter Stadian Edn., 1912 ; various contributions to journalism on track athletics.
ACOLATSE, C. S.—b. 1899 ; ed. Livingston College, Salisbury, U.S.A., and Cambridge Univ.; barrister-at-law, Lincoln’s Inn ; dist. mag., G.C., 1943 ; puisne judge, 1952.

ADAMS, C. O.—b. 1907 ; ed. Forensic Sci. Coll., Nottingham, and Met. Police Detective Trng. Coll., Hendon ; mil. serv., 1939-42, capt.; police cadet, J’ca, 1930 ; asst. supt., 1932 ; asst. comsnr., police, Nig., 1935 ; supt., Trin., 1939 ; sp. br., Scotland Yd., 1949 ; supt., constab., N. Borneo, 1949 ; dep. comsnr., Sarawak constab., 1951.
ADAMS, H. L.—b. 1910; ed. Haileybury ; mil. serv., 1939-45 ; cadet, Ken., 1946 ; sec., comrce. and industry, 1949.

ADDINGTON, J. T. A. P.—b. 1914 ; ed. Downside Sch. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford ; asst. traff. supt., K.U.R. & H., 1949 ; dist. traff. supt., 1947 ; asst. supt., E.A.R. & H., 1950 ; asst. ch. operating supt., 1952 ; chmn., Uga. nat. parks comtee, 1949.

ADDIS, W., C.M.G. (1948), Brill. Star (3rd cl.), Zanz. (1946).—b. 1901 ; ed. Rugby and Magdalene Coll., Camb.; mil. serv., 1939-45, lieut., Zanz. N.V.F.; asst. dist. comsnr., Zanz., 1924 ; secon. to D.O., 1933 ; dist. comsnr., Zanz., 1933 ; dist. offr., N. Rhod., 1936 ; dist. comsnr., Zanz., 1938 ; col. sec., Berm., 1945 ; ag. gov., Oct., 1945-May, 1946, and Mar.-Oct., 1949 ; dep. comsnr.-gen. (col. affrs.), S.E. Asia, 1950 ; gov. and c.in-c., Sey., 1953.

ADDISON, W. R. L., M.C.—b. 1919 ; ed. Haileybury Coll. and Queen’s Coll., Oxford ; mil. serv., 1939-46, maj.; cadet, Uganda, 1948 ; A.D.O., 1950 ; asst. sec., E.A.H.C., 1952 ; clk., cent. leg. assembly, 1951.

ADEMOLA, A. A.—b. 1906 ; ed. King’s Coll., Lagos, and Camb. Univ.; barrister-at-law ; mag., Nig., 1939 ; puisne judge, 1949 ; mem. of comsns. for revision of cts. legn. (1948) and to enq. into Enugu disturbances (1949), Nig.

ADIE, J. J.—b. 1913 ; ed. Kingsland Grange Prep. Sch., Shrewsbury Sch. and Magdalen Coll., Oxford ; mil. serv., 1940-42 (K.A.R. and O.E.T.A.) ; admin. offr., Zanz., 1937 ; asst. dist. comsnr., Pemba, 1938 ; Zanz., 1939 ; p.s. to Br. Res., 1939-40 ; asst. sec., 1942 ; senr. ast. sec. and p.s. to H.H. the Sultan, 1946 ; awarded Brill. Star of Zanz. (4th cl.), 1947 ; secon. C.O., 1949 ; sec., educ. and labr., Ken., 1952 ; author of various articles on Zanzibar.

ADU, A. L., O.B.E. (1953).—b. 1914 ; ed. Presb. Church Schls., Boso and Anum, Achimota Coll. and Sch., and Queen’s Coll., Cambridge ; master, Achimota Coll. and Sch., 1939 ; asst. dist. comsnr., G.C., 1942 ; comsnr. for Africanization, 1950 ; admin. offr., cl. I, 1952.
AGOSTINI, C.—b. 1914; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Trin.; control offr., Trin., 1941; dep. aerodrome supt., 1946; dir. of civil aviation, 1947.

AIKMAN, R. G., C.M.G.—b. 1905; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Bristol Univ.; admin. cadet, Sarawak, 1926; dist. offr., 1936; res., 1940; civ. intern., 1941-45; dep. ch. sec., 1947; ch. sec., 1950; O.A.G., 1952.
AINLEY, A. J., M.C.—b. 1906; ed. St. Bees Sch. and Oxford Univ.; barrister-at-law; mil. serv., 1939-45, maj.; dist. mag., G.C., 1935; cr. coun., 1936; puisne judge, Uga., 1946.
AITKEN, I. H.—b. 1919; ed. Greenock High Sch. and St. Andrews Univ.; tax assessor, Ken., 1944; senr. immigr. offr., 1948; dep. prin. immigr. offr., 1952.

AITKEN, Maj. R.—b. 1910; ed. Gregg Comm. Coll., Ipswich; mil. serv., 1939-46, major; H.M. Prison Service, 1936; secon. supt., prisons, B. Hond., 1946; resig.; N.Z. Prisons Serv., 1947; re-appointed supt., prisons, B. Hond., 1949; dep. dir. of prisons, J'ca., 1951.
ALBURY, K. H. E.—b. 1920; ed. Govt. High Sch., Nassau; clk., public treasy., Bah., 1940; asst. audr., 1950.

ALCOCK, A. E. S.—b. 1902; ed. County High Sch., Ilford, and Lond. Univ.; apptd. Ceylon, 1928; Tang., 1930; town engrn., Kumasi, G.C., 1936; sec. and exec. offr., town and country planning bd., 1945; town plan. advr., 1952; co-author How to Plan your Village; pp. for U.N.O., etc., on stabilized soil building and town planning.

ALCOCK, W. G.—b. 1907; ed. Oxford Prep. Sch., Glasgow Acad. and Glasgow Univ.; solr. (Scotland); asst. lands offr., Tang., 1935; lands offr. and registr.-gen., Nyasa., 1939; off. assignee, S'pore, 1948; comsnr. of estate duties and stamps in addition.
ALDRIDGE, A. S., M.C., Cross of Valour (Poland)—b. 1918; ed. Durham Sch.; mil. serv., 1939-46, maj.; admin. offr., Cyp., 1946; cl. II, 1951; cl. I, 1953.
ALEMA, G. N.—b. 1902; ed. Sierra Leone Gram. Sch., Fourah Bay Coll. and Univ. of Oxford; dep. registr., co-op. soc. (consumers dev.), G.C., 1948.

ALEXANDER, W. G., M.B.E.—b. 1918; ed. Sedbergh Sch. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; mil. serv., 1940-45, major; admin. cadet, G. & E. Is. Col., 1946; co-op. soc. offr., 1947; registr., co-op. societies, Maur., 1951; survey and Report on possibilities co-op. dev. and necessary co-op. legislation in G. & E. Is., 1947.
ALEXIS, F. C.—b. 1913; ed. Grenada Boys' Secondary Sch. and Royal Coll. of Surgeons, Edin.; dist. med. offr., St. V., 1935; supt. physician, col. hosp., Grenada, 1952.

ALFORD, R. E., C.M.G.—b. 1904; ed. Winchester and Univ. Coll., Oxford; nav. serv., 1940-45; cadet, Nig., 1928; asst. dist. offr., 1931; dist. offr. (clk., exec. and leg. couns.), 1938; senr. dist. offr., 1946; fin. sec., Zanz., 1947; ch. sec., 1952.

ALLAN, W., C.B.E. (1954), O.B.E.—b. 1904; ed. North Kelvinside Sch., Glasgow Univ. and I.C.T.A.; asst. research offr., agric. dept., N. Rhod., 1928; asst. dir., agric. and entom., 1938; dir., agric., Maur., 1948.
ALLEN, D. M., M.B.E. (1949).—b. 1916; ed. Bloxham and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; cadet, admin., G.C., 1938; asst. dist. comsnr., 1939; dist. comsnr., 1945; admin. offr., cl. II, 1950; cl. I, 1952.

ALLEN, E. C., O.B.E.—b. 1902; ed. Bradford Commercial Sch. and Sch. of Accountancy; clk., sec't, and reporter, leg. co., Nyasa., 1925; asst. treas., Tang., 1930; senr. 1940; secon. as treas., ch. of customs, British Som., 1940; fin. sec. and collr. of inc. tax, Basuto., 1945.

ALLEN, J. G. C.—b. 1904; ed. Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, Ecole Superieure de Commerce, Lausanne, and Munich Univ.; mil. serv., 1940-43, maj.; cadet, Nig., 1926; senr. dist. offr., 1945; res., 1947; Br. comsnr., Anglo-French Cameroons Boundary Coms., 1937-39; Nig. govt. rep. to Free French, Duala, 1940; i/c. mil. intell. and censorship, Nig., 1940-41; liaison offr. to Gen. Leclerc, 1942; mem., res. min's staff, Accra, 1943; labour offr., 1943-44; off. "goodwill" visit, Algeria and Morocco, 1949.

ALLEN, J. W. T.—b. 1904; ed. Westminster and St. John's Coll., Oxford; supt., educ., T.T., 1929; asst. dist. offr., 1937; dist. offr., 1942; political offr., Aden, 1947; dep. Br. agt., W. Aden Prot., 1948; D.O., Tang., 1953.

ALLEYNE, K. H. C.—b. 1918; ed. Dominica Gram. Sch., Univ. Coll., London, and Queen's Coll., Oxford (Devonshire 2nd course); barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn; registr., Dominica, 1943; mag., St. L., 1944; cr. atty., 1949; St. V., 1952; author of Memoir of constitutional development of St. Lucia, 1950.
ALLIN, P. W. M.—b. 1906; ed. Hilton Coll., Natal, and Rhodes Univ., Cape; land survr., N. Rhod., 1930; senr. survr., 1945; dep. dir., surv. and land, 1951.

AMOROSO-CENTENO, A. E. B., M.B.E.—b. 1910; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Port-of-Spain; called to bar, Gray's Inn; clk., postal, treas. and acctnt.-gen's depts., Trin., 1928-42; assessor, int. rev. dept., 1943; sec., 1950; dep. comsnr., 1952.

ANDERSON, A. H. G.—b. 1901; ed. privately; p.s. to gov., B. Hond., 1927; asst. contrlr., food and prices, 1939; contrlr., 1940; dist. comsnr., 1942; asst. sec. and archaeol. comsnr., 1953; author of Brief Sketch of B. Honduras; British Honduras and articles on archaeology and history.

ANDERSON, R. E.—b. 1903; ed. Guardbridge Sch., Madras Coll., and St. Andrews Univ.; health offr., Mal., 1930; admin. M.O., super-scale, gr. B, 1948; gr. A, 1950; asst. D.M.S., Mal., 1952; ag. D.M.S., 1953.

ANDERSON, T. F., O.B.E. (mil.)—b. 1901; ed. Rugby, Trinity Hall, Camb., and Univ. Coll. Hosp.; mil. serv., 1939-46, col. (desps.); med. offr., Ken., 1928; D.M.S., Som., 1946; D.D.M.S., Ken., 1949; D.M.S., 1949; author of various papers on medicine and health in E. Africa.
ANDRE, J. A. H., M.B.E.—b. 1898; ed. Primary Sch., Maur., Royal Coll., Maur., and L.S.H.T.M.; govt. med. offr., 1924; D.D.M.S., Maur., 1951.
ANDREW-BLAMEY, W. H. J.—b. 1918; ed. Probus Sch., Truro; mil. serv., 1939-46; col. audit serv., Nig., 1946; St. H., 1948; Ken., 1952; col. treas. and collr., cust., St. H., 1952.
ANGUS, J. R.—b. 1912; ed. Malvern Coll., Glas. High Sch. and Edin. Univ.; mil. serv., 1940-41, lt.; asst. consvr., forests, Ken., 1940-50; consvr., forests, Fiji, 1951.

APTHORP, C. H. F., M.V.O., C.P.M.—b. 1904; tpr., Swaz. police, 1924; asst. comsnr., police, Nig., 1927; comsnr. (later senr. asst. supt.), 1936; supt. (later asst. comsnr.), S.L., 1944; comsnr., mounted police, Basuto., 1946; comsnr., police, Nyasa., 1949.

ARCHER, C. V. H.—b. 1904; ed. Harrison Coll., Barb., and Camb. Univ.; barrister-at-law (Gray's Inn); clk. to atty.-gen., Barb., 1930; police mag., Barb., 1935; judge, Bridgetown petty debt ct., 1938; leg. dfismn., Trin., 1944; solr.-gen., 1953; jt. author, 1944, rev. edn. of Laws of Barb.
ARCHER, E. W. D.—b. 1893; ed. Harrison Coll., Barb.; rev. offr., St. V., 1917; asst. mstr., St. Mary's Coll., St. L., 1918; Grenada Boys' Sec. Sch., 1929; hdmstr., Sec. Sch., Montserrat, 1946.

ARCHER, W. S.—b. 1907; ed. The Lodge Sch., Barb.; clk. to the admnr., Dominica, 1931; treas., Antigua, 1941; emergency offr., 1942; fed. supply offr., 1945; asst. col. sec., 1946; prin. asst. sec., Trin., 1948.

ARDEN-CLARKE, Sir Charles, G.C.M.G. (1952), K.C.M.G. (1948), Kt. (1946), C.M.G. (1941).—b. 1898; ed. Rossall Sch.; mil. serv., 1917-20, capt.; admin. offr., Nig., 1920; asst. res. comsnr. and govt. sec., Bech., 1936; res. comsnr., 1937; Basuto., 1942; gov. and c-in-c., Sarawak, 1946; G.C., 1949.

ARDILL, R. H.—b. 1911; ed. Kilkenny Coll., Mountjoy Sch. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; mil. serv., 1942; history mstr., Royal Coll., Maur., 1936; P.R.O., 1946; dep. dir., educ., 1951; author of School Geography of Mauritius.

ARMITAGE, A. H.—b. 1911; ed. Marlborough and Camb. Univ.; asst. audr., Uga., 1935; Leeward Is., 1938; Wind. Is., 1940; audr., B. Hond., 1943; prin. audr., Nig., 1946; G.C., 1951; dep. dir., aud., S'pore, 1953.

ARMITAGE, R. P., C.M.G., M.B.E.—b. 1906; ed. Winchester Coll. and New Coll., Oxford; cadet, Ken., 1929; sec. to mem. for agric., animal husb. and nat. resources, 1945; admin. sec., 1947; und. sec., G.C., 1948; fin. sec., 1948; min. of fin., 1951; gov. and c-in-c., Cyp., 1954.
ARMSTRONG, A. C. F.—b. 1907; ed. St. Edmund's Coll., Ware and Christ's Coll., Camb.; cadet, W. Pac., 1929; Nig., 1941; cl. II., 1949; cl. I., 1953.

ARMSTRONG, G. J., C.M.G., C.B.E. (1946).—b. 1901; ed. St. Andrew's Coll., Grahamstown; clk., sec't., Basuto., 1920; asst. dist. comsnr., 1936; dist. comsnr., 1938; finan. sec., Swaz., 1940; dep. res. comsnr. and govt. sec., Basuto., 1948; agent for S.A.H.C. terrs. in Jo'burg., 1953.
ARMSTRONG, H. C.—b. 1906; ed. Dungannon Royal Sch. and Queen's Univ., Belfast; mil. serv., 1939-43; med. offr., G.C., 1934; M.O.H., 1937; asst. dir., med. services, 1949.
ARMSTRONG, H. H.—b. 1902; ed. Marlborough Coll.; cadet, Tang., 1926; asst. comsnr., labr., Maur., 1940; prov. labr. offr., Nyasa., 1948; senr., 1949; labr. advr., 1952; comsnr., labr., 1953.
ARMSTRONG, R., O.B.E. (1953).—b. 1906; clk., Swaz., 1925; asst. dist. comsnr., 1931; dist. comsnr., 1936; first asst. sec., 1951.

ARNE, Miss B. L.—b. 1900; ed. S.A. and Ashby de la Zouche Girls' Gram. Sch.; min. food and min. supply, 1939; asst. soc. welf. offr., Barb., 1945; soc. welf. offr., 1949; org. sec., nat. jt. comtee. for Spanish relief, 1939.

ARNOLD, R. M.—b. 1918; ed. Cranbrook Sch. and Royal Vet. Coll., Lond., 1938-42; advisory service, min. of agric., 1942; seconded to J'ca as vet. invest. offr., 1946; dep. dir. of agric. (livestock), J'ca, 1950; supt., livestock serv., 1953; repd. J'ca at several techl. conferences; author of Tick Control Measures.

ARROWSMITH, E. P., C.M.G. (1950).—b. 1909; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; asst. dist. comsnr., Bech. Prot., 1933, resig. 1938; comsnr., Turks and Caicos Is., 1940; adminr., Dominica, 1946; res. comsnr., Basuto., 1951.

ARTHUR, O. R., C.M.G., C.V.O.—b. 1905; ed. Charterhouse and Corpus Christi Coll., Camb.; cadet, Nig., 1928; admin. offr., Cyp., 1937; comsnr., 1946; ch. comsnr., 1948; gov's dep., 1950; col. sec., Berm., 1951; gov. and c-in-c. Falk. Is., 1954.

ARUNDELL, Brig. Sir Robert, K.C.M.G. (1950), C.M.G. (1947), O.B.E. (mil.) (1943), K.St.J.—b. 1904; ed. Blundell's Sch. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; mil. serv., 1941-45 (desps., O.B.E.); cadet, Tang., 1927; seconded to C.O., 1935-37; asst. ch. sec., Uga., 1939; ch. civ. affrs. offr., M.E.F., 1944; Br. res. mem. in Washington of Caribbean comsnr., 1946; gov. and c-in-c., Wind. Is., 1948; Barb., 1953; sec., Nyasa. fin. comsnr., 1937-38.
ASH, W. G.—b. 1918; ed. Dover County Sch.; nav. serv., 1939-46; asst. P.R.O., S.L., 1946; P.R.O., 1950.

ASHMORE, J. H., K.P.M., C.P.M.—b. 1900; mil. serv., 1918-19 and 1940-41; sub-inspr., police, B. Guiana, 1920; dist. inspr., 1923; travelling mag. and warden, nos. 3 and 4 mining dists., 1923-24; county inspr., 1927; local comdt., C.I.D., Cyp., 1932; dep. comsnr., police, 1935; comsnr., 1940; air raids comdt., 1940-44; comsnr., Cyp. dist., St. John Ambulance bde.
ASKWITH, T. G.—b. 1911; ed. Haileybury and Cambridge; dist. offr., Ken., 1936; munici. African affrs. offr., 1945; comsnr., community dev., 1950.

ASTLEY, B. A.—b. 1902; ed. St. Albans Sch. and Birkbeck Coll., Univ of Lond.; mil. serv., 1940, capt.; asst. mstr., educ. dept., Ken., 1929; inspr., schs., 1933; hdmstr., Prince of Wales Sch., 1938; ch. inspr., schs., 1944; dep. dir., educ., 1951.
ASTON, F. W.—b. 1903; ed. Kingsmead Sch., Hoylake, Liverpool Univ., Royal (Dick) Vet. Coll., Edin., and Manchester Univ.; vet. offr., Ken., 1928; senr., 1946; D.D.V.S., G.C., 1949.
ATKINS, C. F.—b. 1907; ed. Blundell's Sch., Oundle Sch. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; cadet, Ken., 1930; senr. dist. comsnr., 1950.

ATKINSON, G. A.—b. 1915; ed. Radley Coll., Bartlett Sch. of Archit. and Univ. Coll., Lond.; mil. serv., 1941-46, fit.-lt.; asst. archt., miners' welf. comsnr., 1938; archt., bldg. research stn. D.S.I.R., 1946; senr. archt. and col. liaison offr., 1948; housing advr. to C.O. in addn., 1952; enq. into govt. offrs. housing, Mal., 1952, 1953; consultant, U.N.E.S.C.O. regl. symposium on bldg. research, Delhi, 1952; led U.K. del., C.C.T.A. African housing research conf., Pretoria, 1952; edtr., Colonial Building Notes; guest edtr., U.N. Housing Bulletin No. 6: Housing in the Tropics; author, various tech. pp. on tropical housing and bldg.

ATKINSON, J. B., K.P.M. (1950).—b. 1907; ed. Perrins Sch. and Imp. Coll., Lond.; interned 1942-45; police cadet (chart. co. serv.), N. Borneo, 1929; asst. supt., armed constab., 1933; supt., 1936; comdt., N.B.A.C. and col. police serv., 1946; title changed to comsnr., police, 1950.

ATKINSON, J. C.—b. 1895; ed. Addingham High Prov. Sch., Ilkley Gram. Sch. and Midland Rlyw.; traff. inspr., govt. rlyw., J'ca, 1920; ch. clk. traff. br., 1930; traff. supt., 1938; traff. man., 1943; gen. man. 1949.
ATKINSON, Maj. J. T.—b. 1916; ed. Rutherford Coll., Newcastle; nat. fire serv., 1939-47, column offr.; supt., Georgetown fire bgde., 1947.

ATTENBOROUGH, J.P., C.B.E. (1953), O.B.E. (1946).—b. 1901; ed. Manchester Gram. Sch. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; supt. educ., Nig., 1924; lectr., senr. inspr., Pal., 1930; dir., educ., Aden, 1937; dep. dir., Pal., 1946; asst. educ. advr., C.O., 1948; dir., educ., Tang., 1948.
ATTENBOROUGH, T., M.B.E.—b. 1906; asst. supt., prisons, Sarawak, 1950; supt., 1953.
AUSTIN, A. J.—b. 1907; ed. Clifton Coll.; asst. treas., N. Rhod., 1930; acctnt., 1938; dep. acctnt.-gen., 1939; fin. sec., Swaz., 1945; dep. acctnt.-gen., N. Rhod., 1950; acctnt.-gen., 1951.
AUSTIN, A. M. I.—b. 1914; ed. Dauntsey's Sch.; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple; mil. serv., 1939-45; M.C.S., 1946; C.L.S., fed. coun., Mal., 1948; leg. advr., Pahang, 1951; leg. sec., Malta, 1953.
AZIZ, A.—b. 1900; ed. Turkish Sch. and English Sch., Nicosia; student clk., Cyp., 1918; asst. P.M.G., 1947; P.M.G., 1950.

BACKHOUSE, M. V., M.B.E. (mil.).—b. 1901; ed. Durham Sch. and Oriol Coll., Oxford; mil. serv., 1941-45, maj.; cadet, Nig., 1925; senr. dist. offr., 1944; res., admin. serv., cl. I, 1946; senr. res., 1951.

BACON, R. S., M.B.E. (1943).—b. 1895; ed. Rugby and Balliol Coll., Oxford; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1923; mil. serv., 1914-19 and (staff list) 1940-46, col.; pvt. prac., London, 1923-39; ch. just., Gib., 1946; carried out police enquiry, Gib., 1946; author of Commercial Arbitrations.

BAFFOUR, R. P., O.B.E. (1953).—b. 1912; ed. Mfantsipim Sch. and Achimota Coll.; asst. loco. supt., G.C. rlyw., 1939; lectr., Achimota Coll., 1941; mech. engnr., Accra munic., 1946; ch. trans. offr., 1953.
BAILEY, A. R.—b. 1906; ed. Merchant Taylors' Sch., Univ. Coll., Lond. Univ. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; cadet, Nig., 1929; admin. offr., cl. II, 1947; admin. sec., N. region, 1952.
BAILEY, C. E. S.—b. 1896; ed. St. Kitts Gram. Sch. and Edin. Univ.; govt. M.O., Antigua, 1933; asst. D.M.S. (curative), B. Guiana, 1950.
BAILEY, F. W.—b. 1902; ed. Davenport High Sch.; asst. storekeeper, P.W.D., Nig., 1928; storekeeper, 1937; ch., 1943; contrlr., stores and trans., N. Rhod., 1949.
BAIN, I. C. I.—b. 1896; ed. Grenada Boys' Sec. Sch.; clk., Grenada, 1918; cust. and excise offr., 1924; 2nd clk., treasy., 1931; inspr., inc. tax., 1939; compt., 1944.

BAIRAMIAN, V. R.—b. 1900; ed. English Sch., Nicosia, Cyp., and Univ. Coll., Lond.; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1923; clk., dist. ct., Cyp., 1926; asst. registr., sup. ct., 1927; registr. dist. ct., 1929; also official recvr. in bankruptcy, 1931-39; ch. registr., sup. ct., 1931; asst. dir., land regist., 1938; legal ass't., lands and survey dept., Nig., 1944; ch. registr., sup. ct., 1944; mag., 1946; puisne judge, 1949; sec., usury enq. comtee., Cyp., 1926-28; asst. to atty.-gen., for revision of lands law, 1943.

BAIRD, D. A.—b. 1909; ed. Glasgow Acad., Geo. Watson's Coll., Edin., and Edin. Univ.; med. offr. i/c Church of Scotland Mission Hosp., Blantyre, Nyasa, 1935; col. med. serv., 1943; S.M.O., Som., 1949; D.M.S., 1950.
